v.CNV: 耶和华照着以前所说的，眷顾撒拉；耶和华按着他的应许，给撒拉作成。
v.ESV: The Lord visited Sarah as he had said, and the Lord did to Sarah as he had promised.
v.GAE: 여호와께서 말씀하신 대로 사라를 돌보셨고 여호와께서 말씀하신 대로 사라에게 행하셨으므로
v.KJV: And the LORD visited Sarah as he had said, and the LORD did unto Sarah as he had spoken.
v.NASB: Then the LORD took note of Sarah as He had said, and the LORD did for Sarah as He had promised.
v.NIV: Now the Lord was gracious to Sarah as he had said, and the Lord did for Sarah what he had promised.
v.NRSV: The LORD dealt with Sarah as he had said, and the LORD did for Sarah as he had promised.
v.SAENEW: 주님께서는 말씀하신 대로 사라를 돌보셨다. 사라에게 약속하신 것을 주님께서 그대로 이루시니,
v.WLB: 여호와께서 말씀하신 대로 사라를 돌아보셨고 여호와께서 말씀하신 그대로 사라에게 이뤄 주셔서

v.CNV: 亚伯拉罕年老的时候，撒拉怀了孕，到了　神应许的日期，就给亚伯拉罕生了一个儿子。
v.ESV: And Sarah conceived and bore Abraham a son in his old age at the time of which God had spoken to him.
v.GAE: 사라가 임신하고 하나님이 말씀하신 시기가 되어 노년의 아브라함에게 아들을 낳으니
v.KJV: For Sarah conceived, and bare Abraham a son in his old age, at the set time of which God had spoken to him.
v.NASB: So Sarah conceived and bore a son to Abraham in his old age, at the appointed time of which God had spoken to him.
v.NIV: Sarah became pregnant and bore a son to Abraham in his old age, at the very time God had promised him.
v.NRSV: Sarah conceived and bore Abraham a son in his old age, at the time of which God had spoken to him.
v.SAENEW: 사라가 임신하였고, 하나님이 아브라함에게 약속하신 바로 그 때가 되니, 사라와 늙은 아브라함 사이에서 아들이 태어났다.
v.WLB: 사라가 임신했습니다. 그리고 하나님께서 아브라함에게 약속하신 바로 그때 늙은 아브라함과 사라 사이에서 아들이 태어났습니다.

v.CNV: 亚伯拉罕给撒拉为他所生的儿子，起名叫以撒。
v.ESV: Abraham called the name of his son who was born to him, whom Sarah bore him, Isaac.
v.GAE: 아브라함이 그에게 태어난 아들 곧 사라가 자기에게 낳은 아들을 이름하여 이삭이라 하였고
v.KJV: And Abraham called the name of his son that was born unto him, whom Sarah bare to him, Isaac.
v.NASB: Abraham called the name of his son who was born to him, whom Sarah bore to him, Isaac.
v.NIV: Abraham gave the name Isaac to the son Sarah bore him.
v.NRSV: Abraham gave the name Isaac to his son whom Sarah bore him.
v.SAENEW: 아브라함은 사라가 낳아 준 아들에게 이삭이라는 이름을 지어 주었다.
v.WLB: 아브라함은 자기에게 태어난 아들, 곧 사라가 자기에게 낳아 준 아들을 이삭이라고 이름 지었습니다.

v.CNV: 亚伯拉罕的儿子以撒生下来第八天，亚伯拉罕就照着　神吩咐他的，给以撒行了割礼。
v.ESV: And Abraham circumcised his son Isaac when he was eight days old, as God had commanded him.
v.GAE: 그 아들 이삭이 난 지 팔 일 만에 그가 하나님이 명령하신 대로 할례를 행하였더라
v.KJV: And Abraham circumcised his son Isaac being eight days old, as God had commanded him.
v.NASB: Then Abraham circumcised his son Isaac when he was eight days old, as God had commanded him.
v.NIV: When his son Isaac was eight days old, Abraham circumcised him, as God commanded him.
v.NRSV: And Abraham circumcised his son Isaac when he was eight days old, as God had commanded him.
v.SAENEW: 이삭이 태어난 지 여드레 만에, 아브라함은, 하나님이 분부하신 대로, 그 아기에게 할례를 베풀었다.
v.WLB: 하나님께서 자기에게 명령하신 대로 아브라함은 자기 아들 이삭이 태어난 지 8일 만에 그에게 할례를 베풀었습니다.

v.CNV: 他儿子以撒出生的时候，亚伯拉罕正一百岁。
v.ESV: Abraham was a hundred years old when his son Isaac was born to him.
v.GAE: 아브라함이 그의 아들 이삭이 그에게 태어날 때에 백 세라
v.KJV: And Abraham was an hundred years old, when his son Isaac was born unto him.
v.NASB: Now Abraham was one hundred years old when his son Isaac was born to him.
v.NIV: Abraham was a hundred years old when his son Isaac was born to him.
v.NRSV: Abraham was a hundred years old when his son Isaac was born to him.
v.SAENEW: 아브라함이 아들 이삭을 보았을 때에, 그의 나이는 백 살이었다.
v.WLB: 아들 이삭이 태어났을 때 아브라함의 나이는 100세였습니다.

v.CNV: 撒拉说：“　神使我欢笑，凡听见的，也必为我欢笑。”
v.ESV: And Sarah said, “God has made laughter for me; everyone who hears will laugh over me.”
v.GAE: 사라가 이르되 하나님이 나를 웃게 하시니 듣는 자가 다 나와 함께 웃으리로다
v.KJV: And Sarah said, God hath made me to laugh, so that all that hear will laugh with me.
v.NASB: Sarah said, 'God has made laughter for me; everyone who hears will laugh with me.'
v.NIV: Sarah said, “God has brought me laughter, and everyone who hears about this will laugh with me.”
v.NRSV: Now Sarah said, “God has brought laughter for me; everyone who hears will laugh with me.”
v.SAENEW: 사라가 혼자서 말하였다. "하나님이 나에게 웃음을 주셨구나. 나와 같은 늙은이가 아들을 낳았다고 하면, 듣는 사람마다 나처럼 웃지 않을 수 없겠지."
v.WLB: 사라가 말했습니다. “하나님께서 나로 웃게 하시니 이 소식을 듣는 사람이 다 나와 함께 웃을 것이다.”

v.CNV: 又说：“谁能告诉亚伯拉罕，撒拉要乳养儿女呢？在他年老的时候，我竟给他生了一个儿子。”
v.ESV: And she said, “Who would have said to Abraham that Sarah would nurse children? Yet I have borne him a son in his old age.”
v.GAE: 또 이르되 사라가 자식들을 젖먹이겠다고 누가 아브라함에게 말하였으리요마는 아브라함의 노경에 내가 아들을 낳았도다 하니라
v.KJV: And she said, Who would have said unto Abraham, that Sarah should have given children suck? for I have born him a son in his old age.
v.NASB: And she said, 'Who would have said to Abraham that Sarah would nurse children? Yet I have borne him a son in his old age.'
v.NIV: And she added, “Who would have said to Abraham that Sarah would nurse children? Yet I have borne him a son in his old age.”
v.NRSV: And she said, “Who would ever have said to Abraham that Sarah would nurse children? Yet I have borne him a son in his old age.”
v.SAENEW: 그는 말을 계속하였다. "사라가 자식들에게 젖을 물리게 될 것이라고, 누가 아브라함에게 말할 엄두를 내었으랴? 그러나 내가 지금, 늙은 아브라함에게 아들을 낳아 주지 않았는가!"
v.WLB: 그녀가 말했습니다. “사라가 자식들에게 젖을 먹일 것이라고 누가 아브라함에게 말할 수 있었겠는가? 그러나 내가 늙은 아브라함에게 아들을 낳아 주었도다.”

v.CNV: 孩子渐渐长大，就断了奶。以撒断奶的那天，亚伯拉罕摆设盛大的筵席。
v.ESV: And the child grew and was weaned. And Abraham made a great feast on the day that Isaac was weaned.
v.GAE: 아이가 자라매 젖을 떼고 이삭이 젖을 떼는 날에 아브라함이 큰 잔치를 베풀었더라
v.KJV: And the child grew, and was weaned: and Abraham made a great feast the same day that Isaac was weaned.
v.NASB: The child grew and was weaned, and Abraham made a great feast on the day that Isaac was weaned.
v.NIV: The child grew and was weaned, and on the day Isaac was weaned Abraham held a great feast.
v.NRSV: The child grew, and was weaned; and Abraham made a great feast on the day that Isaac was weaned.
v.SAENEW: 아기가 자라서, 젖을 떼게 되었다. 이삭이 젖을 떼는 날에, 아브라함이 큰 잔치를 벌였다.
v.WLB: 그 아이가 자라 젖을 뗐습니다. 이삭이 젖 떼던 날 아브라함은 큰 잔치를 베풀었습니다.

v.CNV: 撒拉看见那埃及人夏甲给亚伯拉罕所生的儿子在嬉戏，就对亚伯拉罕说：
v.ESV: But Sarah saw the son of Hagar the Egyptian, whom she had borne to Abraham, laughing.
v.GAE: 사라가 본즉 아브라함의 아들 애굽 여인 하갈의 아들이 이삭을 놀리는지라
v.KJV: And Sarah saw the son of Hagar the Egyptian, which she had born unto Abraham, mocking.
v.NASB: Now Sarah saw the son of Hagar the Egyptian, whom she had borne to Abraham, mocking.
v.NIV: But Sarah saw that the son whom Hagar the Egyptian had borne to Abraham was mocking,
v.NRSV: But Sarah saw the son of Hagar the Egyptian, whom she had borne to Abraham, playing with her son Isaac.
v.SAENEW: 그런데 사라가 보니, 이집트 여인 하갈과 아브라함 사이에서 태어난 아들이 이삭을 놀리고 있었다.
v.WLB: 그런데 사라가 보니 아브라함과 이집트 여인 하갈 사이에서 태어난 아들이 이삭을 놀리고 있었습니다.

v.CNV: “把这婢女和她的儿子赶出去，因为这婢女的儿子不能和我的儿子以撒一同承受产业。”
v.ESV: So she said to Abraham, “Cast out this slave woman with her son, for the son of this slave woman shall not be heir with my son Isaac.”
v.GAE: 그가 아브라함에게 이르되 이 여종과 그 아들을 내쫓으라 이 종의 아들은 내 아들 이삭과 함께 기업을 얻지 못하리라 하므로
v.KJV: Wherefore she said unto Abraham, Cast out this bondwoman and her son: for the son of this bondwoman shall not be heir with my son, even with Isaac.
v.NASB: Therefore she said to Abraham, 'Drive out this maid and her son, for the son of this maid shall not be an heir with my son Isaac.'
v.NIV: and she said to Abraham, “Get rid of that slave woman and her son, for that woman’s son will never share in the inheritance with my son Isaac.”
v.NRSV: So she said to Abraham, “Cast out this slave woman with her son; for the son of this slave woman shall not inherit along with my son Isaac.”
v.SAENEW: 사라가 아브라함에게 말하였다. "저 여종과 그 아들을 내보내십시오. 저 여종의 아들은 나의 아들 이삭과 유산을 나누어 가질 수 없습니다."
v.WLB: 사라가 아브라함에게 말했습니다. “저 여종과 그 아들을 내쫓아 버리세요. 저 여종의 아들은 결코 제 아들 이삭과 함께 상속을 받을 수 없습니다.”

v.CNV: 亚伯拉罕因他的儿子而非常烦恼。
v.ESV: And the thing was very displeasing to Abraham on account of his son.
v.GAE: 아브라함이 그의 아들로 말미암아 그 일이 매우 근심이 되었더니
v.KJV: And the thing was very grievous in Abraham’s sight because of his son.
v.NASB: The matter distressed Abraham greatly because of his son.
v.NIV: The matter distressed Abraham greatly because it concerned his son.
v.NRSV: The matter was very distressing to Abraham on account of his son.
v.SAENEW: 그러나 아브라함은, 그 아들도 자기 아들이므로, 이 일로 마음이 몹시 괴로웠다.
v.WLB: 아브라함은 그 아들도 자기 아들이기 때문에 무척 괴로웠습니다.

v.CNV: 神对亚伯拉罕说：“不要因这童子和你的婢女而烦恼。撒拉对你说的话，你都要听从；因为以撒生的，才可以称为你的后裔。
v.ESV: But God said to Abraham, “Be not displeased because of the boy and because of your slave woman. Whatever Sarah says to you, do as she tells you, for through Isaac shall your offspring be named.
v.GAE: 하나님이 아브라함에게 이르시되 네 아이나 네 여종으로 말미암아 근심하지 말고 사라가 네게 이른 말을 다 들으라 이삭에게서 나는 자라야 네 씨라 부를 것임이니라
v.KJV: And God said unto Abraham, Let it not be grievous in thy sight because of the lad, and because of thy bondwoman; in all that Sarah hath said unto thee, hearken unto her voice; for in Isaac shall thy seed be called.
v.NASB: But God said to Abraham, 'Do not be distressed because of the lad and your maid; whatever Sarah tells you, listen to her, for through Isaac your descendants shall be named.
v.NIV: But God said to him, “Do not be so distressed about the boy and your slave woman. Listen to whatever Sarah tells you, because it is through Isaac that your offspring will be reckoned.
v.NRSV: But God said to Abraham, “Do not be distressed because of the boy and because of your slave woman; whatever Sarah says to you, do as she tells you, for it is through Isaac that offspring shall be named for you.
v.SAENEW: 하나님이 그에게 말씀하셨다. "그 아들과 그 어머니인 여종의 일로 너무 걱정하지 말아라. 이삭에게서 태어나는 사람이 너의 씨가 될 것이니, 사라가 너에게 말한 대로 다 들어 주어라.
v.WLB: 하나님께서 아브라함에게 말씀하셨습니다. “그 아이와 네 여종에 대해 괴로워하지 마라. 사라가 네게 뭐라고 하든 그 말을 들어라. 이삭을 통해 난 사람이라야 네 자손이라고 불려질 것이다.

v.CNV: 至于这婢女的儿子，我也要使他成为一国，因为他也是你的后裔。”
v.ESV: And I will make a nation of the son of the slave woman also, because he is your offspring.”
v.GAE: 그러나 여종의 아들도 네 씨니 내가 그로 한 민족을 이루게 하리라 하신지라
v.KJV: And also of the son of the bondwoman will I make a nation, because he is thy seed.
v.NASB: 'And of the son of the maid I will make a nation also, because he is your descendant.'
v.NIV: I will make the son of the slave into a nation also, because he is your offspring.”
v.NRSV: As for the son of the slave woman, I will make a nation of him also, because he is your offspring.”
v.SAENEW: 그러나 여종에게서 난 아들도 너의 씨니, 그 아들은 그 아들대로, 내가 한 민족이 되게 하겠다."
v.WLB: 그러나 내가 네 여종의 아들도 한 나라를 이루게 하겠다. 그 또한 네 자손이기 때문이다.”

v.CNV: 亚伯拉罕清早起来，拿饼和一皮袋水，给了夏甲，放在她的肩膀上，又把孩子交给她，就打发她离开。夏甲走了，在别是巴的旷野飘荡。
v.ESV: So Abraham rose early in the morning and took bread and a skin of water and gave it to Hagar, putting it on her shoulder, along with the child, and sent her away. And she departed and wandered in the wilderness of Beersheba.
v.GAE: 아브라함이 아침에 일찍이 일어나 떡과 물 한 가죽부대를 가져다가 하갈의 어깨에 메워 주고 그 아이를 데리고 가게 하니 하갈이 나가서 브엘세바 광야에서 방황하더니
v.KJV: And Abraham rose up early in the morning, and took bread, and a bottle of water, and gave it unto Hagar, putting it on her shoulder, and the child, and sent her away: and she departed, and wandered in the wilderness of Beer-sheba.
v.NASB: So Abraham rose early in the morning and took bread and a skin of water and gave them to Hagar, putting them on her shoulder, and gave her the boy, and sent her away. And she departed and wandered about in the wilderness of Beersheba.
v.NIV: Early the next morning Abraham took some food and a skin of water and gave them to Hagar. He set them on her shoulders and then sent her off with the boy. She went on her way and wandered in the Desert of Beersheba.
v.NRSV: So Abraham rose early in the morning, and took bread and a skin of water, and gave it to Hagar, putting it on her shoulder, along with the child, and sent her away. And she departed, and wandered about in the wilderness of Beer-sheba.
v.SAENEW: 다음날 아침에 일찍, 아브라함은 먹거리 얼마와 물 한 가죽부대를 가져다가, 하갈에게 주었다. 그는 먹거리와 마실 물을 하갈의 어깨에 메워 주고서, 그를 아이와 함께 내보냈다. 하갈은 길을 나서서, 브엘세바 빈 들에서 정처없이 헤매고 다녔다.
v.WLB: 다음날 아침 일찍 아브라함은 음식과 물이 담긴 가죽 부대를 가져다 하갈의 어깨에 메어 주고 아이와 함께 떠나보냈습니다. 그녀는 길을 떠나 브엘세바 광야에서 방황했습니다.

v.CNV: 皮袋里的水用尽了，夏甲就把孩子撇在一棵小树底下。
v.ESV: When the water in the skin was gone, she put the child under one of the bushes.
v.GAE: 가죽부대의 물이 떨어진지라 그 자식을 관목덤불 아래에 두고
v.KJV: And the water was spent in the bottle, and she cast the child under one of the shrubs.
v.NASB: When the water in the skin was used up, she left the boy under one of the bushes.
v.NIV: When the water in the skin was gone, she put the boy under one of the bushes.
v.NRSV: When the water in the skin was gone, she cast the child under one of the bushes.
v.SAENEW: 가죽부대에 담아 온 물이 다 떨어지니, 하갈은 아이를 덤불 아래에 뉘어 놓고서
v.WLB: 가죽 부대의 물이 다 떨어지자 하갈은 아이를 덤불 아래 두고

v.CNV: 然后自己走开，在离开约有一箭之远的地方，相对而坐，说：“我不忍眼看孩子死去。”就相对坐着，放声大哭。
v.ESV: Then she went and sat down opposite him a good way off, about the distance of a bowshot, for she said, “Let me not look on the death of the child.” And as she sat opposite him, she lifted up her voice and wept.
v.GAE: 이르되 아이가 죽는 것을 차마 보지 못하겠다 하고 화살 한 바탕 거리 떨어져 마주 앉아 바라보며 소리 내어 우니
v.KJV: And she went, and sat her down over against him a good way off, as it were a bowshot: for she said, Let me not see the death of the child. And she sat over against him, and lift up her voice, and wept.
v.NASB: Then she went and sat down opposite him, about a bowshot away, for she said, 'Do not let me see the boy die.' And she sat opposite him, and lifted up her voice and wept.
v.NIV: Then she went off and sat down about a bowshot away, for she thought, “I cannot watch the boy die.” And as she sat there, she began to sob.
v.NRSV: Then she went and sat down opposite him a good way off, about the distance of a bowshot; for she said, “Do not let me look on the death of the child.” And as she sat opposite him, she lifted up her voice and wept.
v.SAENEW: "아이가 죽어 가는 꼴을 차마 볼 수가 없구나!" 하면서, 화살 한 바탕 거리만큼 떨어져서, 주저앉았다. 그 여인은 아이 쪽을 바라보고 앉아서, 소리를 내어 울었다.
v.WLB: 화살이 날아갈 거리만큼 떨어진 곳에 가서 마주 보고 주저앉았습니다. 그녀는 “이 아이가 죽는 것을 차마 지켜볼 수가 없구나”라고 말하며 아이를 마주 보고 앉아 큰 소리로 울었습니다.

v.CNV: 神听见了童子的声音，他的使者就从天上呼叫夏甲，对她说：“夏甲啊，你为什么这样呢？不要害怕，因为孩子在那里所发的声音　神已经听见了。
v.ESV: And God heard the voice of the boy, and the angel of God called to Hagar from heaven and said to her, “What troubles you, Hagar? Fear not, for God has heard the voice of the boy where he is.
v.GAE: 하나님이 그 어린 아이의 소리를 들으셨으므로 하나님의 사자가 하늘에서부터 하갈을 불러 이르시되 하갈아 무슨 일이냐 두려워하지 말라 하나님이 저기 있는 아이의 소리를 들으셨나니
v.KJV: And God heard the voice of the lad; and the angel of God called Hagar out of heaven, and said unto her, What aileth thee, Hagar? fear not; for God hath heard the voice of the lad where he is.
v.NASB: God heard the lad crying; and the angel of God called to Hagar from heaven and said to her, 'What is the matter with you, Hagar? Do not fear, for God has heard the voice of the lad where he is.
v.NIV: God heard the boy crying, and the angel of God called to Hagar from heaven and said to her, “What is the matter, Hagar? Do not be afraid; God has heard the boy crying as he lies there.
v.NRSV: And God heard the voice of the boy; and the angel of God called to Hagar from heaven, and said to her, “What troubles you, Hagar? Do not be afraid; for God has heard the voice of the boy where he is.
v.SAENEW: 하나님이 그 아이가 우는 소리를 들으셨다. 하늘에서 하나님의 천사가 하갈을 부르며 말하였다. "하갈아, 어찌 된 일이냐? 무서워하지 말아라. 아이가 저기에 누워서 우는 저 소리를 하나님이 들으셨다.
v.WLB: 하나님께서 그 아이가 우는 소리를 들으셨습니다. 하나님의 천사가 하늘에서 하갈을 불러 말했습니다. “하갈아, 네게 무슨 일이 있느냐? 하나님께서 저기 있는 아이의 소리를 들으셨으니 두려워하지 마라.

v.CNV: 起来，扶起孩子，尽力保护他；因为我要使他成为大国。”
v.ESV: Up! Lift up the boy, and hold him fast with your hand, for I will make him into a great nation.”
v.GAE: 일어나 아이를 일으켜 네 손으로 붙들라 그가 큰 민족을 이루게 하리라 하시니라
v.KJV: Arise, lift up the lad, and hold him in thine hand; for I will make him a great nation.
v.NASB: 'Arise, lift up the lad, and hold him by the hand, for I will make a great nation of him.'
v.NIV: Lift the boy up and take him by the hand, for I will make him into a great nation. ”
v.NRSV: Come, lift up the boy and hold him fast with your hand, for I will make a great nation of him.”
v.SAENEW: 아이를 안아 일으키고, 달래어라. 내가 저 아이에게서 큰 민족이 나오게 하겠다."
v.WLB: 이제 일어나 가서 저 아이를 일으켜 그 손을 잡아라. 내가 그로 큰 나라를 이루게 하겠다.”

v.CNV: 神开了夏甲的眼睛，她就看见一口水井。她去把皮袋盛满了水，就给孩子喝。
v.ESV: Then God opened her eyes, and she saw a well of water. And she went and filled the skin with water and gave the boy a drink.
v.GAE: 하나님이 하갈의 눈을 밝히셨으므로 샘물을 보고 가서 가죽부대에 물을 채워다가 그 아이에게 마시게 하였더라
v.KJV: And God opened her eyes, and she saw a well of water; and she went, and filled the bottle with water, and gave the lad drink.
v.NASB: Then God opened her eyes and she saw a well of water; and she went and filled the skin with water and gave the lad a drink.
v.NIV: Then God opened her eyes and she saw a well of water. So she went and filled the skin with water and gave the boy a drink.
v.NRSV: Then God opened her eyes and she saw a well of water. She went, and filled the skin with water, and gave the boy a drink.
v.SAENEW: 하나님이 하갈의 눈을 밝히시니, 하갈이 샘을 발견하고, 가서, 가죽부대에 물을 담아다가 아이에게 먹였다.
v.WLB: 하나님께서 하갈의 눈을 밝게 하시니 그녀가 샘 하나를 발견했습니다. 그녀가 가서 가죽 부대에 물을 채워 아이에게 마시게 했습니다.

v.CNV: 神与孩子同在，他渐渐长大，住在旷野，成了个弓箭手。
v.ESV: And God was with the boy, and he grew up. He lived in the wilderness and became an expert with the bow.
v.GAE: 하나님이 그 아이와 함께 계시매 그가 장성하여 광야에서 거주하며 활 쏘는 자가 되었더니
v.KJV: And God was with the lad; and he grew, and dwelt in the wilderness, and became an archer.
v.NASB: God was with the lad, and he grew; and he lived in the wilderness and became an archer.
v.NIV: God was with the boy as he grew up. He lived in the desert and became an archer.
v.NRSV: God was with the boy, and he grew up; he lived in the wilderness, and became an expert with the bow.
v.SAENEW: 그 아이가 자라는 동안에, 하나님이 그 아이와 늘 함께 계시면서 돌보셨다. 그는 광야에 살면서, 활을 쏘는 사람이 되었다.
v.WLB: 하나님께서 그 아이와 함께 계시는 가운데 그는 자라났습니다. 그는 광야에서 살면서 활 쏘는 사람이 됐습니다.

v.CNV: 他住在巴兰的旷野，他的母亲从埃及地给他娶了一个妻子。
v.ESV: He lived in the wilderness of Paran, and his mother took a wife for him from the land of Egypt.
v.GAE: 그가 바란 광야에 거주할 때에 그의 어머니가 그를 위하여 애굽 땅에서 아내를 얻어 주었더라
v.KJV: And he dwelt in the wilderness of Paran: and his mother took him a wife out of the land of Egypt.
v.NASB: He lived in the wilderness of Paran, and his mother took a wife for him from the land of Egypt.
v.NIV: While he was living in the Desert of Paran, his mother got a wife for him from Egypt.
v.NRSV: He lived in the wilderness of Paran; and his mother got a wife for him from the land of Egypt.
v.SAENEW: 그가 바란 광야에서 살 때에, 그의 어머니가 그에게 이집트 땅에 사는 여인을 데려가서, 아내로 삼게 하였다.
v.WLB: 그는 바란 광야에서 살았습니다. 그의 어머니가 그를 위해 이집트 여자를 그의 아내로 맞아들였습니다.

v.CNV: 那时，亚比米勒和他的军长非各对亚伯拉罕说：“在你所作的一切事上，　神都与你同在。
v.ESV: At that time Abimelech and Phicol the commander of his army said to Abraham, “God is with you in all that you do.
v.GAE: 그 때에 아비멜렉과 그 군대 장관 비골이 아브라함에게 말하여 이르되 네가 무슨 일을 하든지 하나님이 너와 함께 계시도다
v.KJV: And it came to pass at that time, that Abimelech and Phichol the chief captain of his host spake unto Abraham, saying, God is with thee in all that thou doest:
v.NASB: Now it came about at that time that Abimelech and Phicol, the commander of his army, spoke to Abraham, saying, 'God is with you in all that you do;
v.NIV: At that time Abimelek and Phicol the commander of his forces said to Abraham, “God is with you in everything you do.
v.NRSV: At that time Abimelech, with Phicol the commander of his army, said to Abraham, “God is with you in all that you do;
v.SAENEW: 그 무렵에 아비멜렉과 그의 군사령관 비골이 아브라함에게 말하였다. "하나님은, 당신이 무슨 일을 하든지, 당신을 도우십니다.
v.WLB: 그때 아비멜렉과 그의 군대 사령관 비골이 아브라함에게 말했습니다. “그대가 하는 모든 일에 하나님께서 그대와 함께하십니다.

v.CNV: 现在你要在这里指着　神对我起誓，你不会以诡诈待我和我的子子孙孙。我怎样恩待了你，你也要怎样恩待我和你寄居的地方。”
v.ESV: Now therefore swear to me here by God that you will not deal falsely with me or with my descendants or with my posterity, but as I have dealt kindly with you, so you will deal with me and with the land where you have sojourned.”
v.GAE: 그런즉 너는 나와 내 아들과 내 손자에게 거짓되이 행하지 아니하기를 이제 여기서 하나님을 가리켜 내게 맹세하라 내가 네게 후대한 대로 너도 나와 네가 머무는 이 땅에 행할 것이니라
v.KJV: Now therefore swear unto me here by God that thou wilt not deal falsely with me, nor with my son, nor with my son’s son: but according to the kindness that I have done unto thee, thou shalt do unto me, and to the land wherein thou hast sojourned.
v.NASB: now therefore, swear to me here by God that you will not deal falsely with me or with my offspring or with my posterity, but according to the kindness that I have shown to you, you shall show to me and to the land in which you have sojourned.'
v.NIV: Now swear to me here before God that you will not deal falsely with me or my children or my descendants. Show to me and the country where you now reside as a foreigner the same kindness I have shown to you.”
v.NRSV: now therefore swear to me here by God that you will not deal falsely with me or with my offspring or with my posterity, but as I have dealt loyally with you, you will deal with me and with the land where you have resided as an alien.”
v.SAENEW: 이제 여기 하나님 앞에서, 당신이 나와 나의 아이들과 나의 자손을 속이지 않겠다고 맹세하십시오. 당신이 나그네살이를 하는 우리 땅에서, 내가 당신에게 한 것처럼, 당신도 나와 이 땅 사람들에게 친절을 베풀어 주시기 바랍니다."
v.WLB: 그러니 그대가 이제 나나 내 자식이나 내 자손들을 속이지 않겠다고 여기 하나님 앞에서 내게 맹세하십시오. 내가 그대에게 친절을 베푼 것처럼 그대도 내게 또 그대가 이방 사람으로 살고 있는 이 땅에 호의를 베푸시기 바랍니다.”

v.CNV: 亚伯拉罕回答：“我愿意起誓。”
v.ESV: And Abraham said, “I will swear.”
v.GAE: 아브라함이 이르되 내가 맹세하리라 하고
v.KJV: And Abraham said, I will swear.
v.NASB: Abraham said, 'I swear it.'
v.NIV: Abraham said, “I swear it.”
v.NRSV: And Abraham said, “I swear it.”
v.SAENEW: 아브라함이 말하였다. "맹세합니다."
v.WLB: 아브라함이 말했습니다. “제가 맹세합니다.”

v.CNV: 亚伯拉罕因亚比米勒的仆人强占了一口水井，就指责亚比米勒。
v.ESV: When Abraham reproved Abimelech about a well of water that Abimelech's servants had seized,
v.GAE: 아비멜렉의 종들이 아브라함의 우물을 빼앗은 일에 관하여 아브라함이 아비멜렉을 책망하매
v.KJV: And Abraham reproved Abimelech because of a well of water, which Abimelech’s servants had violently taken away.
v.NASB: But Abraham complained to Abimelech because of the well of water which the servants of Abimelech had seized.
v.NIV: Then Abraham complained to Abimelek about a well of water that Abimelek’s servants had seized.
v.NRSV: When Abraham complained to Abimelech about a well of water that Abimelech’s servants had seized,
v.SAENEW: 이렇게 말하고 나서, 아브라함은, 아비멜렉의 종들이 우물을 빼앗은 것을 아비멜렉에게 항의하였다.
v.WLB: 그러고 나서 아브라함은 아비멜렉의 종들이 빼앗은 우물에 대해 항의했습니다.

v.CNV: 亚比米勒说：“谁作了这事，我不知道，你都没有告诉我；我现在才听见。”
v.ESV: Abimelech said, “I do not know who has done this thing; you did not tell me, and I have not heard of it until today.”
v.GAE: 아비멜렉이 이르되 누가 그리하였는지 내가 알지 못하노라 너도 내게 알리지 아니하였고 나도 듣지 못하였더니 오늘에야 들었노라
v.KJV: And Abimelech said, I wot not who hath done this thing: neither didst thou tell me, neither yet heard I of it, but to day.
v.NASB: And Abimelech said, 'I do not know who has done this thing; you did not tell me, nor did I hear of it until today.'
v.NIV: But Abimelek said, “I don’t know who has done this. You did not tell me, and I heard about it only today.”
v.NRSV: Abimelech said, “I do not know who has done this; you did not tell me, and I have not heard of it until today.”
v.SAENEW: 그러나 아비멜렉은 이렇게 말하였다. "누가 그런 일을 저질렀는지, 나는 모릅니다. 당신도 그런 말을 여태까지 나에게 하지 않았습니다. 나는 그 일을 겨우 오늘에 와서야 들었습니다."
v.WLB: 그러자 아비멜렉이 말했습니다. “누가 이렇게 했는지 나는 모릅니다. 당신도 내게 이 일에 대해 지금까지 말해 주지 않았습니다. 나는 오늘에야 비로소 이 일에 대해 들었습니다.”

v.CNV: 于是亚伯拉罕牵了牛羊来，送给亚比米勒，二人就彼此立约。
v.ESV: So Abraham took sheep and oxen and gave them to Abimelech, and the two men made a covenant.
v.GAE: 아브라함이 양과 소를 가져다가 아비멜렉에게 주고 두 사람이 서로 언약을 세우니라
v.KJV: And Abraham took sheep and oxen, and gave them unto Abimelech; and both of them made a covenant.
v.NASB: Abraham took sheep and oxen and gave them to Abimelech, and the two of them made a covenant.
v.NIV: So Abraham brought sheep and cattle and gave them to Abimelek, and the two men made a treaty.
v.NRSV: So Abraham took sheep and oxen and gave them to Abimelech, and the two men made a covenant.
v.SAENEW: 아브라함이 양과 소를 끌고 와서, 아비멜렉에게 주고, 두 사람이 서로 언약을 세웠다.
v.WLB: 아브라함이 양과 소를 가져다가 아비멜렉에게 주고 그 두 사람이 언약을 맺었습니다.

v.CNV: 亚伯拉罕把羊群中的七只母羊羔，放在另外一处。
v.ESV: Abraham set seven ewe lambs of the flock apart.
v.GAE: 아브라함이 일곱 암양 새끼를 따로 놓으니
v.KJV: And Abraham set seven ewe lambs of the flock by themselves.
v.NASB: Then Abraham set seven ewe lambs of the flock by themselves.
v.NIV: Abraham set apart seven ewe lambs from the flock,
v.NRSV: Abraham set apart seven ewe lambs of the flock.
v.SAENEW: 아브라함이 양 떼에서 새끼 암양 일곱 마리를 따로 떼어 놓으니,
v.WLB: 아브라함이 새끼 암양 일곱 마리를 따로 떼어 놓았습니다.

v.CNV: 亚比米勒问亚伯拉罕：“你把这七只母羊羔放在另外一处，是什么意思呢？”
v.ESV: And Abimelech said to Abraham, “What is the meaning of these seven ewe lambs that you have set apart?”
v.GAE: 아비멜렉이 아브라함에게 이르되 이 일곱 암양 새끼를 따로 놓음은 어찜이냐
v.KJV: And Abimelech said unto Abraham, What mean these seven ewe lambs which thou hast set by themselves?
v.NASB: Abimelech said to Abraham, 'What do these seven ewe lambs mean, which you have set by themselves?'
v.NIV: and Abimelek asked Abraham, “What is the meaning of these seven ewe lambs you have set apart by themselves?”
v.NRSV: And Abimelech said to Abraham, “What is the meaning of these seven ewe lambs that you have set apart?”
v.SAENEW: 아비멜렉이 아브라함에게 물었다. "새끼 암양 일곱 마리를 따로 떼어 놓은 까닭이 무엇입니까?"
v.WLB: 아비멜렉이 아브라함에게 물었습니다. “당신은 왜 새끼 암양 일곱 마리를 따로 놓았습니까?”

v.CNV: 亚伯拉罕回答：“意思就是：你从我手里接受这七只母羊羔，好作我挖了这口井的证据。”
v.ESV: He said, “These seven ewe lambs you will take from my hand, that this may be a witness for me that I dug this well.”
v.GAE: 아브라함이 이르되 너는 내 손에서 이 암양 새끼 일곱을 받아 내가 이 우물 판 증거를 삼으라 하고
v.KJV: And he said, For these seven ewe lambs shalt thou take of my hand, that they may be a witness unto me, that I have digged this well.
v.NASB: He said, 'You shall take these seven ewe lambs from my hand so that it may be a witness to me, that I dug this well.'
v.NIV: He replied, “Accept these seven lambs from my hand as a witness that I dug this well. ”
v.NRSV: He said, “These seven ewe lambs you shall accept from my hand, in order that you may be a witness for me that I dug this well.”
v.SAENEW: 아브라함이 대답하였다. "내가 이 우물을 파 놓은 증거로, 이 새끼 암양 일곱 마리를 드리려고 합니다."
v.WLB: 아브라함이 대답했습니다. “이 새끼 암양 일곱 마리를 제 손에서 받으십시오. 이것으로 제가 이 우물을 판 증거로 삼으십시오.”

v.CNV: 因此那地方名叫别是巴，因为他们二人在那里起了誓。
v.ESV: Therefore that place was called Beersheba, because there both of them swore an oath.
v.GAE: 두 사람이 거기서 서로 맹세하였으므로 그 곳을 브엘세바라 이름하였더라
v.KJV: Wherefore he called that place Beer-sheba; because there they sware both of them.
v.NASB: Therefore he called that place Beersheba, because there the two of them took an oath.
v.NIV: So that place was called Beersheba, because the two men swore an oath there.
v.NRSV: Therefore that place was called Beer-sheba; because there both of them swore an oath.
v.SAENEW: 이 두 사람이 여기에서 이렇게 맹세를 하였으므로, 그 곳을 브엘세바라고 한다.
v.WLB: 거기서 이 두 사람이 맹세했기 때문에 그 장소를 브엘세바라고 불렀습니다.

v.CNV: 他们在别是巴立约以后，亚比米勒和他的军长非各，就起程回非利士地去了。
v.ESV: So they made a covenant at Beersheba. Then Abimelech and Phicol the commander of his army rose up and returned to the land of the Philistines.
v.GAE: 그들이 브엘세바에서 언약을 세우매 아비멜렉과 그 군대 장관 비골은 떠나 블레셋 사람의 땅으로 돌아갔고
v.KJV: Thus they made a covenant at Beer-sheba: then Abimelech rose up, and Phichol the chief captain of his host, and they returned into the land of the Philistines.
v.NASB: So they made a covenant at Beersheba; and Abimelech and Phicol, the commander of his army, arose and returned to the land of the Philistines.
v.NIV: After the treaty had been made at Beersheba, Abimelek and Phicol the commander of his forces returned to the land of the Philistines.
v.NRSV: When they had made a covenant at Beer-sheba, Abimelech, with Phicol the commander of his army, left and returned to the land of the Philistines.
v.SAENEW: 아브라함과 아비멜렉이 브엘세바에서 언약을 세운 다음에, 아비멜렉과 그의 군사령관 비골은 블레셋 사람의 땅으로 돌아갔다.
v.WLB: 브엘세바에서 언약을 맺고 난 후 아비멜렉과 그의 군대 사령관 비골은 블레셋 족속의 땅으로 돌아갔습니다.

v.CNV: 亚伯拉罕在别是巴栽了一棵垂丝柳树，就在那里求告耶和华永活的　神的名。
v.ESV: Abraham planted a tamarisk tree in Beersheba and called there on the name of the Lord , the Everlasting God.
v.GAE: 아브라함은 브엘세바에 에셀 나무를 심고 거기서 영원하신 하나님 여호와의 이름을 불렀으며
v.KJV: And Abraham planted a grove in Beer-sheba, and called there on the name of the LORD, the everlasting God.
v.NASB: Abraham planted a tamarisk tree at Beersheba, and there he called on the name of the LORD, the Everlasting God.
v.NIV: Abraham planted a tamarisk tree in Beersheba, and there he called on the name of the Lord , the Eternal God.
v.NRSV: Abraham planted a tamarisk tree in Beer-sheba, and called there on the name of the LORD, the Everlasting God.
v.SAENEW: 아브라함은 브엘세바에 에셀 나무를 심고, 거기에서, 영생하시는 주 하나님의 이름을 부르며 예배를 드렸다.
v.WLB: 아브라함은 브엘세바에 에셀 나무를 심고 그곳에서 영원하신 하나님 여호와의 이름을 불렀습니다.

v.CNV: 亚伯拉罕在非利士人的地方寄居了许多日子。
v.ESV: And Abraham sojourned many days in the land of the Philistines.
v.GAE: 그가 블레셋 사람의 땅에서 여러 날을 지냈더라
v.KJV: And Abraham sojourned in the Philistines’ land many days.
v.NASB: And Abraham sojourned in the land of the Philistines for many days.
v.NIV: And Abraham stayed in the land of the Philistines for a long time.
v.NRSV: And Abraham resided as an alien many days in the land of the Philistines.
v.SAENEW: 아브라함은 오랫동안 블레셋 족속의 땅에 머물러 있었다.
v.WLB: 아브라함은 블레셋 족속의 땅에서 오래 머물러 살았습니다.
